briefdescription.para: This is the Error handler callback function and this function increments the the error counter so that the main thread knows the number of errors.

parameterdescription.para: is a reference to the Axi Ethernet device instance.

briefdescription.para: This is the Receive handler function for examples 1 and 2.

detaileddescription.para: It will increment a shared counter, receive and validate the frame.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DMA ring instance.

briefdescription.para: This is the DMA TX Interrupt handler function.

detaileddescription.para:
TxRingPtr
is a pointer to TX channel of the DMA engine.
None.
This Interrupt handler MUST clear pending interrupts before handling them by calling the call back. Otherwise the following corner case could raise some issue: A packet got transmitted and a TX interrupt got asserted. If the interrupt handler calls the callback before clearing the interrupt, a new packet may get transmitted in the callback. This new packet then can assert one more TX interrupt before the control comes out of the callback function. Now when eventually control comes out of the callback function, it will never know about the second new interrupt and hence while clearing the interrupts, would clear the new interrupt as well and will never process it. To avoid such cases, interrupts must be cleared before calling the callback.

briefdescription.para: This function demonstrates the usage usage of the Axi Ethernet extended multicast feature.

detaileddescription.para: It uses AXI DMA core in interrupt mode to transfer Ethernet frames.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the AXIDMA component.
contains the base address of the AxiEthernet device

simplesect.para: AxiDma hardware must be initialized before initializing AxiEthernet. Since AxiDma reset line is connected to the AxiEthernet reset line, a reset of AxiDma hardware during its initialization would reset AxiEthernet.

briefdescription.para: This example sends and receives a single packet in loopback mode with extended multicast address support.

detaileddescription.para: The transmit frame will be addressed one of programmed multicast addresses.
On receive, HW should pass the frame to receive interrupt handler for further address checking.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the AXI DMA component.

briefdescription.para: AxiEthernet_AddExtMulticast adds the multicast Ethernet address table in DDR.

detaileddescription.para: This table is mainly for software driver to perform the final address filtering.

Once an Ethernet address is programmed, the Axi Ethernet channel will begin receiving data sent from that address. The way to prevent the Axi Ethernet channel from receiving messages from an Ethernet address in the DDR table is to clear it with AxiEthernet_ClearExtMulticast().

AddressPtr
is a pointer to the 6-byte Ethernet address to set.
- XST_SUCCESS,on successful completion
XST_INVALID_PARAM, if input MAC address is not between 01:00:5E:00:00:00 and 01:00:5E:7F:FF:FF, as per RFC1112.
This table is independent to BRAM table that is used for HW address filtering. It is user's responsiblility to maintain these tables.

In the multicast table, hardware requires it to be 'indexed' with bit 22-8, 15 bits in total of mac address. This address filtering is done in hardware and provisioned with XAxiEthernet_[Add|Clear]ExtMulticastSet() APIs.

This routine consider all 2**23 possible multicast ethernet addresses to be 8Mx1 bit or 1M bytes memory area. All defined multicast addresses are from 01.00.5E.00.00.00 to 01.00.5E.7F.FF.FF The first 25 bit out of 48 bit are static, so they will not be part of calculation.

In memory, software used every bit represents every defined MAC address, then we have this formula for table lookup. offset + (address >> 3) => memory location. address % 32 => bit location for multicast address Let's take 01:00:5E:00:01:FF(hex), memory location : 0x3F bit location : 0x1F

briefdescription.para: AxiEthernet_ClearExtMulticast clears the Ethernet address in multicast address table in DDR.

detaileddescription.para:
AddressPtr
is a pointer to the 6-byte Ethernet address to set.
- XST_SUCCESS,on successful completion
XST_INVALID_PARAM, if input MAC address is not between 01:00:5E:00:00:00 and 01:00:5E:7F:FF:FF, as per RFC1112.
Please reference AxiEthernet_AddExtMulticast for multicast ethernet address index and bit value calculation.

briefdescription.para: AxiEthernet_GetExtMulticast verifies the Ethernet address in multicast address table in DDR.

detaileddescription.para:
AddressPtr
is a pointer to the 6-byte Ethernet address to set.
-TRUE, if AddressPtr is provisioned. -FALSE, if AddressPtr is not provisioned.
Please reference AxiEthernet_AddExtMulticast for multicast ethernet address index and bit value calculation.

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for the Axi Ethernet example.

detaileddescription.para: This function is not included if the example is generated from the TestAppGen test tool.

briefdescription.para: This is the DMA TX callback function to be called by TX interrupt handler.

detaileddescription.para: This function handles BDs finished by hardware.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to TX channel of the DMA engine.

briefdescription.para: This is the DMA RX callback function to be called by RX interrupt handler.

detaileddescription.para: This function handles finished BDs by hardware, attaches new buffers to those BDs, and give them back to hardware to receive more incoming packets

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to RX channel of the DMA engine.

briefdescription.para: Implements examples that utilize the AXI Ethernet's interrupt driven DMA packet transfer mode to send and receive multicast frames when XAE_EXT_MULTICAST_OPTION is enabled.

listitem.para: Which interrupt BD word are verified and confirm it is a valid multicast frame.
AxiEthernetSgDmaIntrExtMulticast Example demonstrates the extended multicast. The HW must be setup for extended multicast for this example to execute.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a asa 4/30/10 First release based on the ll temac driver 1.01a asa 12/10/10 Changes made to enable the AXIDMA Tx/Rx ring interrupts before allocation of Tx/Rx BDs. 3.00a asa 6/25/12 Modified XAxiDma_BdSetLength API call to support new AXI DMA driver version 7.00a. 3.00a bss 10/22/12 Added support for Fast Interrupt Handlers. 3.01a srt 02/14/13 Added support for Zynq (CR 681136) 5.4 ms 01/23/17 Modified xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. ms 04/05/17 Added tabspace for return statements in functions for proper documentation while generating doxygen. 5.7 rsp 19/12/17 Defined Padding and ExternalLoopback variables.
